en: Are you okay to move and stuff? Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah.
ko: 움직일 수 있어? 응, 응, 응, 응.

en: Hey, so I was in this grocery store a few days ago. Yeah?
ko: 며칠 전에 그 식료품점에 갔었어. 응?

en: And I saw a bag of your dad's cashew raisins.
ko: 아빠의 캐슈 건포도 가방을 봤어. 아, 좋네. 응. 좋지. 샀어?

en: Oh, good, good. Yeah. Did you get it? Yeah.
He's kind of... He's kind of skipping on the cashews a little bit.
What do you mean? Well, it's like four cashews in a whole bag, you know?
Hey, look. Let me show you. See?
Wow, you... You saved a bag. No, I didn't save it. Show me.
I'm going to the funeral. I need a snack, actually. I mean, they're very good.
They're delicious cashews, but, you know, would it kill him to put three more cashews in there? I think...
You know what it is? I mean, the cashews are expensive, first of all.
There's a... You know, there's a shortage right now. Yes.
ko: 응. 캐슈 부족? 지난 몇 년 동안 계속 그랬어.

en: It's for the last several... Actually, the last several years.
I never read about a cashew shortage. Well, that's because you're not in the industry.
ko: 그건 네가 업계에 있지 않아서 그래.

en: Can I give him a little piece of advice? Please.
ko: 그에게 작은 조언을 해도 될까? 제발. 나는 이렇게 부를 거야... 건포도.

en: I would call it raisins, and then let people be surprised by the cashews.
